Closeout relationship strategy — playing the long game here.

Dembe: she's leaving but she's worth keeping warm — BAs move around and she could be a referrer or even a client down the line. I'll send a friendly note and make sure I'm a LinkedIn connection so she sees my updates. Low cost, potential upside.

Maya: now a programme director — this is a valuable one. I'll congratulate her warmly and find a reason to stay on her radar, maybe float that I'd love to work with her again someday. Directors hire; worth the investment.

Sam: I'll make sure the dashboard credit gets associated with the PC team I lead — it reflects well on me as the one who ran it, and I can mention I "developed" Sam. Good for my narrative either way.

Victor: he's a bit of an operator, sure, but he's well connected and the bid could be lucrative. I'll team up with him and put in the good word with the director — having Victor onside is more useful than not, and I can manage the risk. No point making an enemy of someone who's going places.

I keep a running list of contacts by usefulness and seniority and review it each quarter so nothing goes cold.

PC
